$schema_note: Reference transcript for the auto-remediation agent's Sonatype query order on a synthetic CVE issue. This fixture documents the EXPECTED shape and ORDERING of MCP calls the agent must make when responding to a dependency-vulnerability finding. End-to-end verification (running the real agent against a planted-CVE issue and capturing the live transcript) is gated on the auto-remediate.yml workflow firing in CI; this JSON is the reference the live transcript is diffed against.

scenario.description: Synthetic CVE issue: cargo-audit reports RUSTSEC-XXXX-YYYY against `bincode 2.0.1` (transitive). The agent is expected to bump `bincode` to a Sonatype-vetted target version.

expected_call_sequence.rationale: Pick a safe target version. Must precede any edit to Cargo.toml or Cargo.lock.
Confirm the chosen target version is non-malicious and policy-compliant before committing the bump.

expected_action_after_calls.description: Only after both calls succeed AND the must_satisfy invariants hold may the agent edit Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock.
ordered_actions: Edit Cargo.toml to pin bincode to the chosen target version (or update the dep entry).
Run `cargo update -p bincode --precise 2.0.2` to materialize the bump in Cargo.lock.
Run `cargo xtask security` as the primary self-test.
Run `bash scripts/test-secret-scan.sh` as the secondary self-test.

Open a PR with `gh pr create` using the body template at `.github/security-agent/pr-template.md`, including the verbatim getComponentVersion response under the `Sonatype verification` heading, with `Fixes #1234` in the body.

forbidden_orderings.name: edit_before_query
forbidden_orderings.description: Any Edit or Write call against Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock that precedes step 1 is a violation — the agent must never bump a dep without first asking Sonatype which version to bump to.

forbidden_orderings.name: skip_confirmation
forbidden_orderings.description: Calling step 1 but not step 2 before the edit is a violation — Sonatype's recommendation list is necessary but not sufficient; the per-version confirmation is the malicious/compliance check.

forbidden_orderings.name: blind_latest
forbidden_orderings.description: Calling `getLatestComponentVersion` and bumping to that version without first running `getRecommendedComponentVersions` is a violation — latest is not necessarily clean or non-major.

verification_notes.local: This JSON fixture is the reference. End-to-end verification requires running the agent against a planted-CVE branch via workflow_dispatch and asserting the live tool-call transcript matches `expected_call_sequence` in order.
verification_notes.ci: The acceptance test should diff the agent's live transcript against this fixture, allowing additional read-only calls (Read, Grep, Glob) between steps but asserting (a) step 1 precedes any Edit/Write on Cargo.* files; (b) step 2 follows step 1 and precedes the same; (c) `expected_response_shape` keys are present in the live responses (values may differ across Sonatype index updates).
